title: Bobotie for dinner
excerpt: I grew up with bobotie. My mother served it to us with peas and yellow rice. Usually there was stewed fruit served as a side. The recipe I'm giving today is the basic recipe. There are various twists to make it even more interesting. I'll …
html: I grew up with bobotie.  My mother served it to us with peas and yellow rice.  Usually there was  stewed fruit served as a side.  The recipe I'm giving today is the basic recipe.  There are various twists to make it even more interesting .  I'll also include those at the bottom of the recipe.

recipe.title: Bobotie for dinner
recipe.author: Marinda Engelbrecht

items: 15 ml oil to fry the onions in
5 ml ground cumin (if you have it, otherwise leave it out)

steps: Put the bread in a deep bowl and pour the 100 ml milk over the bread.
Fry the onions until glassy in a little oil on the stove.
Put all the rest of the ingredients into a mixing bowl and mix thoroughly.
Squeeze the milk out of the bread and mix it together with your onions into the meat mixture.
Spoon it into a greased oven dish.
Mix your topping (1 beaten egg and the 60 ml milk) and pour it over your meat in the oven dish.
Place the lemon leaves on top of everything.
Bake for 60 minutes at 190 degrees celsius.

notes: What you can add: You can stir 125 ml raisins or chopped dried apricots into your meat mixture.
You can sprinkle flaked almonds over your meat mixture.
You can mash pie apples and layer them whole over the meat. Then, on top of the apples, crumble 125ml grated cheese and 200ml flour and sprinkle that over.
Enjoy your bobotie with rice and vegetables. Always a winner, and it will feed quite a few people.
